title: chamaemelum tanacetifolium
intro: Chamaemelum tanacetifolium, also known as Tansy, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its fern-like foliage and yellow, button-like flowers. Traditionally, Tansy has been used in herbal medicine due to its carminative, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in alleviating digestive issues and relieving menstrual cramps. The health benefits of Tansy include its ability to improve digestion, reduce stress, and act as a natural insect repellent. Tansy can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to create a medicinal blend.
